folio.l: manye children , bretheryn , Cosyns , & othere ny kynrede ,

& thougú
so were , æè haddest slayn of he~ , two or thre , ©et dwellen æere
I nowe , to wreken here detú , & to slee thy ¹sone

so be , æ ©oure kynrede be more & stedefast than the kyn of ©ourö
aduäsarie ,

©et natheles , ©oure kynrede , nys but a fer kynrede ,
they ben but litel sibbe to ©ow

¢ And the kyn of ©oure enmys
ben ny sibbe to he~ , And certes as in æ , here condico~n is bet æa~ne
©oures

¢ Tha~ne lat vs co~sidre also , if æe cou~seillyng of he~ , æ
cou~seileden ©ow , to taken sodeyn vengeau~ce , wheither it a corde
to resou~ ,
And certes ©e knowe wel nay ,

For as by rigút & resou~
æäe may noman taken vengeance on no wigút , but the Iuge
æ hatú the Iurisdicco~n of it ,

whan it is I gunted hi~ , to take
thilke vengeance , hastily or attemprely , as the lawe requyretú ,

And ©et more ouä , of thilke word , that Tullius clepetú co~sentyn-
ge ,

æè shalt cou~sidre , if thy mygút & thy powere may consente
and suffise to thy wylfulnesse , & to thy cou~seilloås ,

And certes æè
maist wel seyn , æ nay ,

for sikerly , as for to speke ¸prely , we
moun do no thyngö , but oonly swicú thyngö , as we moun don
rigútfully ,

And certes , rigútfully , ne mowe ©e take no vengean-
ce , as of ©oure ¸pre auctorite

power xx xxink blotch? double checknsentetú nat , ne acordetú nat to ©oure wilfulnesse

lat vs now examyne , the thridde poynt , æ Tullius cle-
petú Consequent

¢ Thow shalt vnderstande , æ the venge-
ance æ æè påposest for to take , is the co~sequent ,

& ther of folwetú
a nother vengeance , ¹ile & werre , & oæäe damages , w oute
noumbre , of whicú we ben nat war , as at this tyme

¢ And
as touchyng the ferthe poynt , æ Tullius clepetú engendry~ge

æè shalt co~sidre , æ this wrong , whicú æ is doon to the , is
engendred , of the hate of thyne enemys ,

& of the vengeance
takyngö , vp on æ , wolde engendre a nother vengeance & muchel
sorwe , & wastynge of richesses , as I seide

¢ Now sire , as to
æe poynt , æ Tullius clepetú causes , whicú æ is the laste po-
ynt ,
æè shalt vnderstonde , it hatú certeyne causes ,

whicú æ
menclerkesmenclerkes not sign of substitution./ODR/ clepen , Oriens , and Efficiens , & causa longinqua , & causa
¸pinqua , this is to seyn ; the fer cause & the ny cause ,

the
fer cause , is , almygúty god , æ is cause of alle thynges ,

the
ner cause , is thyne thre enemys ,

the cause accidental , was
hate ,

the cause material , ben the fyue woundes of thy dougú-
ter

the cause formal , is the manere of here werkynge æ brou©-
ten laddres , & clomben In at thy wyndowes ,

the cause fynal ,
was , for to slee thy dougúter , it letted nat in as muche as in
hem was ,
But for to speke of the fer cause , as to what ende
